  "findings": [
    "Concrete segregation observed due to excessive drop height during placement."
  ],
  "actions": [
    "Halt the concrete placement immediately.",
    "Remove the segregated concrete mix from the forms before it sets.",
    "Adjust the placement method to limit free-fall drop height to a maximum of 3 to 5 feet using drop chutes or tremies."
  ],
  "rationale": "Placing concrete from an excessive height causes the coarse aggregate to separate from the mortar (segregation), violating ACI 301 and 304 standards. This severely compromises the structural integrity and finish of the concrete. The placement must be stopped, the segregated material removed, and the drop height corrected before pouring can resume.",
  "references": [
    "ACI 301 (Specifications for Structural Concrete)",
    "ACI 304R (Guide for Measuring, Mixing, Transporting, and Placing Concrete)"
  ]
}
